name: Franklin Hot Springs
description: Hot springs in the Central Coast region of California.

name: The Geysers
description: Geothermal field in the Mayacamas Mountains. Not a soaking spring — the world's largest geothermal steam field used for power generation.

name: Hot Springs (Surprise Valley)
description: Geothermal hot springs in the remote Surprise Valley of Northeastern California near the Nevada border.

name: Lake City Hot Springs
description: Hot springs near Lake City in Modoc County, Northeastern California.

name: Terminal Geyser
description: Geothermal feature near Lassen Volcanic National Park with extremely hot water. Not recommended for soaking.

name: Wendel Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs in Lassen County near the Nevada border.

name: Morgan Hot Spring
description: Geothermal hot spring near Lassen Volcanic National Park.

name: Seyferth Hot Springs
description: Remote hot springs in Modoc County, Northeastern California.

name: Whitmore Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs in the Eastern Sierra near Mammoth Lakes.

name: Hill Top Hot Springs
description: Free natural hot spring tubs in the Eastern Sierra with panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada.
best_time: Sunrise or sunset for best views

name: Willy's Hot Spring
description: Popular free hot spring along the John Muir Trail near Mammoth Lakes.

name: Crab Cooker Hot Springs
description: Natural rock-walled hot spring near Mammoth Lakes in the Eastern Sierra.

name: Shepard Hot Springs
description: Secluded natural hot spring tub near Mammoth Lakes in the Eastern Sierra.

name: Rock Tub Hot Springs
description: Small natural hot spring tub near Mammoth Lakes in the Eastern Sierra.

name: Little Hot Creek
description: Natural hot creek in the Eastern Sierra near Mammoth Lakes. Hot water mixes with cool creek flow.

name: Red's Meadow Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs near Red's Meadow Resort in the Ansel Adams Wilderness.

name: Iva Bell Hot Springs
description: Remote hot springs in the Ansel Adams Wilderness, accessible via a strenuous hike.
best_time: Late summer when trail is snow-free
insider_tips: Requires a 10+ mile hike. Bring everything you need — no services.

name: Travertine Hot Springs
description: Scenic travertine rock formations with natural soaking pools near Bridgeport in the Eastern Sierra.
insider_tips: The pools closest to the road are the most visited. Walk further for more secluded options.

name: Wilbur Hot Springs
description: Historic resort with naturally flowing mineral springs in Colusa County. Temperature ranges from 98-112°F.

name: Sierra Hot Springs
description: Non-profit retreat center with natural mineral springs in the Sierra Nevada. Temperature ranges from 98-110°F.
insider_tips: Book in advance — it's a popular retreat destination.

name: Big Caliente Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs in the Santa Ynez Mountains behind Santa Barbara.

name: Little Caliente Hot Springs
description: Smaller hot springs near Big Caliente in the Santa Ynez Mountains.

name: Stingleys Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs in the Los Padres National Forest near Santa Barbara.

name: California Hot Springs
description: Historic hot springs community in the Sierra Nevada foothills of Tulare County.

name: Slates Hot Springs
description: Coastal hot springs on the Big Sur coastline. Access requires hiking or boat.

name: Desert Hot Springs
description: Desert community with multiple spa resorts fed by natural mineral hot springs near Palm Springs.

name: Coso Hot Springs
description: Geothermal area within the Naval Air Weapons Station China Lake. Not accessible to the public.

name: Devils Kitchen
description: Geothermal area near Coso Hot Springs within military grounds. Not accessible to the public.

name: Bagby Hot Springs
description: Historic bathhouse in Mt Hood National Forest, 1.4-mile hike to rustic cedar tubs.

name: Breitenbush Hot Springs
description: Retreat center with natural rock pools along the Breitenbush River.

name: Terwilliger (Cougar) Hot Springs
description: Deep turquoise pool in old-growth forest, 0.25-mile hike from trailhead.

name: Umpqua Hot Springs
description: Scenic pools overlooking the Umpqua River, 0.3-mile hike.

name: McCredie Hot Springs
description: Free roadside springs along the North Fork of the Willamette River.

name: Belknap Hot Springs
description: Landscaped pools at Belknap Lodge along the McKenzie River.

name: Carson Hot Springs Resort
description: Historic mineral bathhouse and hotel in the Columbia River Gorge.

name: Saint Martin's Hot Springs
description: Three rock pools along a creek in the Columbia Gorge, short hike.

name: Moffett's Hot Springs
description: Natural pool along the Wind River, hidden forest setting.

name: Kitson Hot Springs
description: Remote springs in the Willamette National Forest along Salt Creek.

name: Alvord Hot Springs
description: Desert springs at the base of Steens Mountain with stunning views.

name: Mickey Hot Springs
description: Geothermal area in the Alvord Desert, pools too hot for soaking in most spots.

name: Crystal Crane Hot Springs
description: Resort with a large outdoor pool and private soaking tubs in Harney County.

name: Hart Mountain Hot Springs
description: Natural rock pool on Hart Mountain National Antelope Refuge.

name: Snively Hot Springs
description: Riverside pool along the Owyhee River in eastern Oregon canyon country.

name: Givens Hot Springs
description: Historic roadside soaking pool along the Snake River in eastern Oregon.

name: Mundo Hot Springs
description: Small resort with covered soaking pool in eastern Oregon ranching country.

name: Paulina Hot Springs
description: Natural pools near East Lake in the Newberry National Volcanic Monument.

name: Three Forks Hot Spring
description: Remote canyon springs at the confluence of three streams in SE Oregon.

name: Willow Creek Hot Springs
description: BLM campground with natural soaking pool in the high desert.

name: Bigelow Hot Spring
description: Small natural pool along the McKenzie River trail.

name: Fisher Hot Springs
description: Warm pool in the high desert, near the Nevada border.

name: Blue Mountain Hot Springs
description: Natural springs in the Blue Mountains of eastern Oregon.

name: Little Geysers
description: Geothermal area near The Geysers — extreme heat, not for soaking.

name: Susanville Hot Spring
description: Hot springs near Susanville in Lassen County.
